title: Commentary on Policy/Limits Compliance: 03-31-2026
content:
The credit union remains compliant in most areas with board-approved policy limits, supported by generally satisfactory asset quality data and capital metrics that are largely within target.

Asset Quality:
Classified assets/Net worth of 2% remain(s) below the limit of 40%, while delinquent loans/Total loans of 0.00% remain(s) above the threshold of 1.5%. Net charge-offs/Average loans of 0.00% also remain(s) within the best practice range.

Capital & Solvency:
Net worth ratio remains an area of concern, with net worth/Total assets at 9%, which falls below the target benchmark of 10%. However, solvency evaluation stands strong at 107%, exceeding its minimum requirement. Net worth growth is also lagging, registering a decline of -8% compared to the annualized target range.

Profitability & Growth:
Net margin/Average assets remains within policy guidelines at 3%, while return on average assets falls below the required threshold of 0.80%. Operating expenses/average assets exceed their limit at 4%, indicating elevated cost pressures relative to asset base. Loan growth is strong, exceeding expectations with a rate above the target range.

Interest Rate & Funding:
Cash and short-term investments as a percentage of total assets remain below preferred ranges at 6% versus an acceptable band between 8-15%. Regular shares/Total shares and borrowings fall significantly under their minimum threshold. Net interest margin/Average assets remains weak, falling below the target level.

Liquidity & Mix:
Cost of funds as a percentage of average assets exceeds its limit at 2%, while total loans to total assets remain above range but within acceptable parameters for liquidity management.
